response: This function implements the classic bottom-up dynamic programming algorithm for the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) problem: it builds an (m+1)×(n+1) table `dp` where `dp[i][j]` is the LCS length of prefixes `a[:i]` and `b[:j]`, extending by one on a character match (`dp[i-1][j-1]+1`) or otherwise taking the maximum of the cell above or to the left. The final entry `dp[m][n]` is the length of the LCS of the full sequences.
